Images (.png, .jpg, .webp) are uploaded as photo attachments, audio as file attachments. You can also include image URLs in markdown format ![alt](url) and they will be uploaded as attachments.

function.name: web_extract
function.description: Extract content from web page URLs. Returns page content in markdown format. Also works with PDF URLs (arxiv papers, documents, etc.) — pass the PDF link directly and it converts to markdown text. Pages under 5000 chars return full markdown; larger pages are LLM-summarized and capped at ~5000 chars per page. Pages over 2M chars are refused. If a URL fails or times out, use the browser tool to access it instead.

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from (max 5 URLs per call)

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web for information. Returns up to 5 results by default with titles, URLs, and descriptions. The query is passed through to the configured backend, so operators such as site:domain, filetype:pdf, intitle:word, -term, and "exact phrase" may work when the backend supports them.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web. You may include backend-supported operators such as site:example.com, filetype:pdf, intitle:word, -term, or "exact phrase".

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 5.

{
  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://github.com/datalab-to/marker",
      "title": "GitHub - datalab-to/marker: Convert PDF to markdown + JSON quickly with high accuracy · GitHub",
      "content": "# Marker (datalab-to/marker) GitHub Repository Summary
---
## Repository Overview
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| GitHub Stars | 34.7k |
| Forks | 2.4k |
| Watchers | 123 |
| Contributors | 29 |
| Releases | 71 |
| Latest Version | v1.10.2 (Jan 2026) |
| Primary Language | Python 99.7% |

> Convert documents to markdown, JSON, chunks, and HTML quickly with high accuracy.

---
## Core Capabilities
✅ **Supported Inputs**: PDF, image, PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, HTML, EPUB (all languages)
✅ Automatically formats: tables, forms, equations, inline math, links, references, code blocks
✅ Extracts and saves embedded images
✅ Removes headers/footers/document artifacts
✅ Fully extensible with custom logic
✅ Beta: structured extraction against user-provided JSON schema
✅ Optional LLM accuracy boost
✅ Runs natively on GPU, CPU, or Apple MPS

---
## Performance & Benchmarks
> Marker benchmarks favorably compared to cloud services like Llamaparse and Mathpix, as well as other open source tools.

### Overall Conversion Benchmark (H100)
| Method | Avg Time / Page | Heuristic Score | LLM Judge Score |
|---|---|---|---|
| Marker | 2.84s | 95.7 | 4.24 |
| Mathpix | 6.36s | 86.4 | 4.16 |
| Docling | 3.70s | 86.7 | 3.70 |
| Llamaparse | 23.35s | 84.2 | 3.98 |

* Projected batch throughput: **25 pages/second on H100**
* Hybrid LLM mode outperforms both standalone Marker and standalone Gemini
* Table extraction: 0.816 base score, **0.907 score with LLM enabled**

---
## Installation
Requires Python 3.10+ & PyTorch
```bash
# Base install (PDF only support)
pip install marker-pdf

# Full install (all supported file types)
pip install marker-pdf[full]
```

---
## Usage
### Interactive GUI
```bash
pip install streamlit streamlit-ace
marker_gui
```

### CLI Commands
```bash
# Convert single file
marker_single /path/to/file.pdf

# Batch convert entire folder
marker /path/to/input/folder

# Multi-GPU distributed batch conversion
NUM_DEVICES=4 NUM_WORKERS=15 marker_chunk_convert ../pdf_in ../md_out
```

#### Common CLI Flags
| Flag | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `--page_range` | Select pages e.g. `"0,5-10,20"` |
| `--output_format` | `markdown` / `json` / `html` / `chunks` |
| `--use_llm` | Enable LLM accuracy boost |
| `--force_ocr` | Full document re-OCR (fixes garbled text, improves math) |
| `--paginate_output` | Add explicit page number markers |
| `--disable_image_extraction` | Skip image saving |
| `--debug` | Enable debug logging + bounding box export |

### Python Usage
```python
from marker.converters.pdf import PdfConverter
from marker.models import create_model_dict
from marker.output import text_from_rendered

converter = PdfConverter(artifact_dict=create_model_dict())
rendered = converter("FILEPATH")
text, _, images = text_from_rendered(rendered)
```

### Specialized Converters
| Converter | Use Case |
|---|---|
| `TableConverter` | Extract only tables from documents |
| `OCRConverter` | Run only OCR processing |
| `ExtractionConverter` | Beta: structured JSON extraction against schema |

---
## Hybrid LLM Mode
Enable with `--use_llm` for highest accuracy. Supported LLM backends:
1.  Google Gemini (default)
2.  Google Vertex
3.  Ollama (local open models)
4.  Anthropic Claude
5.  OpenAI / Azure OpenAI

> The `use_llm` mode offers higher accuracy than marker or gemini alone.

---
## Output Formats
| Format | Details |
|---|---|
| **Markdown** | Proper tables, fenced LaTeX, code blocks, image links, footnotes |
| **HTML** | Standard semantic tags, native math formatting |
| **JSON** | Full hierarchical block tree with bounding boxes and block type metadata |
| **Chunks** | Flat block list optimized directly for RAG usage |
| All outputs include auto-generated TOC, page statistics, and extraction method metadata |

---
## Built-in API Server
Simple FastAPI server for small scale use:
```bash
pip install uvicorn fastapi python-multipart
marker_server --port 8001
```
> Interactive endpoint documentation available at `localhost:8001/docs`

---
## Commercial Usage
* ✅ Free for: research, personal use, startups with < $2M annual revenue/funding
* Code license: GPL-3.0
* Model license: Modified Open Rail-M
* Commercial licenses / GPL removal available via Datalab
* Managed enterprise platform: processes **200M+ pages per week**, SOC 2 Type 2 compliant

---
## Known Limitations
* Complex nested tables/forms may have rendering issues
* Form field extraction can be imperfect
> ✅ Both issues are almost entirely resolved by using `--use_llm` + `--force_ocr`

---
## Troubleshooting
1.  Poor output accuracy: enable `--use_llm`
2.  Garbled/corrupted text: add `--force_ocr`
3.  Out of memory errors: reduce worker count
4.  Device detection issues: manually set `TORCH_DEVICE` environment variable

---
## Additional Resources
* [Public Demo Playground](https://www.datalab.to/playground)
* [Official Documentation](https://documentation.datalab.to/)
* [Discord Community](https://discord.gg/KuZwXNGnfH)
* Modal cloud deployment example included in repository",

      "url": "https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown",
      "title": "GitHub - microsoft/markitdown: Python tool for converting files and office documents to Markdown. · GitHub",
      "content": "# MarkItDown (Microsoft)
> Lightweight Python utility for converting files & office documents to Markdown, optimized for LLM usage and text analysis pipelines.

---

## Repository Overview
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| ⭐ Stars | 121k |
| 🍴 Forks | 8.1k |
| 👀 Watchers | 408 |
| 🛠️ Used by | 2.6k projects |
| ✍️ Contributors | 75 |
| 📦 Latest Release | v0.1.5 (Feb 20, 2026) |
| 🐍 Required Python | >= 3.10 |
| 📜 License | MIT |

*Maintained by the Microsoft AutoGen Team*

---

## Core Details
### Purpose
Converts documents to structured Markdown while preserving headings, lists, tables, links and logical document structure.
> While output is reasonably human-readable, it is **designed for consumption by text analysis tools / LLMs** - this is not intended for high-fidelity document conversion for end users.

### Why Markdown?
1.  Near plain text with minimal overhead markup
2.  Natively understood by all mainstream LLMs
3.  Extremely token efficient for LLM processing

### Supported Input Formats
✅ PDF, Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Excel
✅ Images (EXIF metadata + OCR)
✅ Audio (EXIF metadata + speech transcription)
✅ HTML, CSV, JSON, XML
✅ ZIP archives, Youtube URLs, EPubs, Outlook messages

---

## ⚠️ Critical Security Notice
> MarkItDown performs I/O with the privileges of the current process. Like `open()` or `requests.get()`, it will access resources that the process itself can access.

1.  **Always sanitize all inputs** when running in untrusted / server-side environments
2.  Use the narrowest conversion function required for your use case:
    *   Prefer `convert_stream()` > `convert_local()` > `convert_response()`
    *   Avoid the general permissive `convert()` method with untrusted input
3.  Restrict allowed file paths, URI schemes and network destinations for untrusted usage

---

## Prerequisites
Requires Python 3.10+. Always use an isolated virtual environment:
```bash
# Standard Python venv
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
```
Also supports `uv` and Conda environments.

---

## Installation
### Full installation (all formats):
```bash
pip install 'markitdown[all]'
```

### Selective minimal installation:
Install only dependencies for formats you require:
```bash
pip install 'markitdown[pdf, docx, pptx]'
```

Available optional dependency groups:
`all`, `pptx`, `docx`, `xlsx`, `xls`, `pdf`, `outlook`, `az-doc-intel`, `audio-transcription`, `youtube-transcription`

### Source installation:
```bash
git clone git@github.com:microsoft/markitdown.git
cd markitdown
pip install -e 'packages/markitdown[all]'
```

---

## Usage
### Command Line
```bash
# Basic conversion
markitdown input.pdf > output.md

# Explicit output file
markitdown input.pdf -o output.md

# Pipe input
cat input.pdf | markitdown

# Convert using Azure Document Intelligence
markitdown input.pdf -o output.md -d -e "<document_intelligence_endpoint>"
```

### Python API
```python
from markitdown import MarkItDown

md = MarkItDown(enable_plugins=False)
result = md.convert("test.xlsx")
print(result.text_content)
```

#### LLM Vision / Image Description
```python
from markitdown import MarkItDown
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()
md = MarkItDown(
    llm_client=client,
    llm_model="gpt-4o",
    llm_prompt="optional custom prompt"
)
result = md.convert("example.jpg")
```

### Docker
```bash
docker build -t markitdown:latest .
docker run --rm -i markitdown:latest < your-file.pdf > output.md
```

---

## Plugins
Plugins are **disabled by default**
```bash
# List installed plugins
markitdown --list-plugins

# Run with plugins enabled
markitdown --use-plugins input.pdf
```

### Official OCR Plugin
Extracts text from embedded images inside documents using LLM Vision. No additional ML libraries or binary dependencies required.
```bash
pip install markitdown-ocr openai
```
```python
md = MarkItDown(
    enable_plugins=True,
    llm_client=OpenAI(),
    llm_model="gpt-4o",
)
result = md.convert("document_with_images.pdf")
```

*Search GitHub for `#markitdown-plugin` to find community plugins.*

---

## Contributing
1.  Microsoft Contributor License Agreement (CLA) required for all code contributions
2.  Look for issues/PRs tagged `open for contribution` or `open for reviewing`
3.  Run tests locally:
    ```bash
    cd packages/markitdown
    pip install hatch
    hatch test
    ```
4.  Always run `pre-commit run --all-files` before submitting a PR
5.  Third party plugins are welcome, see the sample plugin template in the repository

---

## Repository Metadata
*   Languages: 99.7% Python, 0.3% Dockerfile
*   Topics: `markdown`, `pdf`, `openai`, `microsoft-office`, `autogen`, `langchain`
*   Code of Conduct: Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct applies",

## Core Value Proposition
> Convert Word, PDF, Excel, PowerPoint, web pages, images, audio, archives, code files, RSS and URLs into Markdown in seconds. No signup required, free forever, private by design.

---

## Key Features
All confirmed functionality:
- 20+ supported file formats: DOCX, PDF, CSV, XLSX, PPTX, HTML, JPG, PNG, MP3, ZIP, IPYNB, JSON, RSS + additional formats
- Multiple input methods: drag & drop, clipboard paste, file browser select, direct URL input
- Batch processing: up to 20 files at once, with optional merge of all outputs into a single Markdown document
- Live Markdown preview, flexible export: single file download or full batch download
- **Privacy first design**: cloud processing with no persistent storage, all transfers encrypted
- Optimized for fast performance on all devices and browsers for professional workflows

---

## Verified Use Cases
1.  **LLM & Retrieval Workflows**
    Convert unstructured documents to standardized Markdown for reliable prompt context, dataset creation, and consistent retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)
2.  **Personal Knowledge Management**
    Convert lecture notes, research, meeting minutes and web clips for import into Obsidian, Notion and other markdown editors; eliminates manual cleanup work and enables full-text search
3.  **Content Publishing & Migration**
    Convert reports, articles and existing website content for fast import into blogs, static site generators and CMS platforms, accelerating cross-platform migration work

---

## Pricing
✅ **100% free forever** | No user account or signup required at any time

      "content": "# Docling Repository Summary
> Open-source document processing library purpose-built for Gen AI. Originally developed at IBM Research Zurich, hosted by the LF AI & Data Foundation.

---

## 📊 Repository Statistics
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| GitHub Stars | **59.3k** |
| Forks | 4.1k |
| Watchers | 212 |
| Contributors | 226 |
| Active Dependents | 3.1k |
| Latest Stable Release | v2.93.0 (May 5, 2026) |
| License | MIT |
| Primary Language | Python 99.3% |

---

## 🎯 Project Purpose
> Docling simplifies document processing, parses diverse formats including advanced PDF understanding, and provides seamless integrations with the generative AI ecosystem.

---

## ✨ Core Capabilities
### Supported Input Formats
- Standard documents: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, LaTeX, plain text, Markdown variants
- Media: Images (PNG/TIFF/JPEG), Audio (WAV/MP3), WebVTT captions
- Specialized schemas: USPTO patents, JATS academic articles, XBRL financial reports

### Key Features
- Advanced PDF parsing: page layout, correct reading order, table structure, code blocks, formulas, image classification
- Fully local execution for sensitive / air-gapped environments
- OCR for scanned documents and images
- Visual Language Model support (including GraniteDocling)
- Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) for audio files
- Chart understanding: converts bar/pie/line charts to structured tables or descriptive text
- Unified typed `DoclingDocument` data representation
- Export formats: Markdown, HTML, WebVTT, DocTags, lossless JSON
- Native Gen AI integrations: LangChain, LlamaIndex, Crew AI, Haystack
- MCP server for agent connectivity
- Standalone CLI interface

### Recent Additions (2026)
✅ Structured information extraction (beta)
✅ `Heron` default layout model for 2x faster PDF parsing
✅ MCP agent server
✅ XBRL financial document parsing
✅ WebVTT parsing & export
✅ Native LaTeX parsing

### Upcoming Features
🔜 Automatic metadata extraction (title, authors, references, language)
🔜 Molecular chemistry structure understanding

---

## 🚀 Quickstart
### 1. Install
```bash
pip install docling
```
> ✅ Compatibility: Python 3.10+, runs on macOS / Linux / Windows (x86_64 + arm64)
> ❌ Python 3.9 support was dropped in v2.70.0

### 2. CLI Usage
Convert any document to structured Markdown:
```bash
docling https://arxiv.org/pdf/2206.01062
```

Run conversion with GraniteDocling VLM pipeline:
```bash
docling --pipeline vlm --vlm-model granite_docling https://arxiv.org/pdf/2206.01062
```

### 3. Python API Usage
```python
from docling.document_converter import DocumentConverter

source = "https://arxiv.org/pdf/2408.09869" # local path or URL
converter = DocumentConverter()
result = converter.convert(source)
print(result.document.export_to_markdown())
```

## 🚀 Core Capabilities
### 1. Intelligent Document Parsing (LLM Ready)
> *Transforming messy visuals into structured data for the LLM era.*
- **PaddleOCR-VL-1.5 (0.9B)**: Industry leading lightweight document VLM
  - Handles real world edge cases: warping, scanning, screen photos, bad lighting, skewed documents
  - Outputs clean Markdown / JSON structured data
  - 94.5% accuracy on OmniDocBench benchmark
- **PP-StructureV3**: Fine-grained coordinate extraction for tables, text positions and layout elements
- Outperforms many closed-source solutions while remaining lightweight for edge/cloud deployment

### 2. Universal Scene OCR
> *Global gold standard for high-speed multilingual text spotting*
- ✅ **100+ languages** natively supported, including mixed language documents
- PP-OCRv5 model: 13% accuracy improvement over prior versions, maintains extreme efficiency
- Works on IDs, street views, books, industrial components and all natural scene text

### 3. Developer Ecosystem
- Native integrations with: **Dify, RAGFlow, Pathway, Cherry Studio, Haystack, MinerU, Umi-OCR**
- One click deployment across all major hardware backends
- Complete dataset pipeline for LLM fine tuning data generation

1.  **Flexible inference backends**: Switch between Paddle static/dynamic graph or Transformers; 20 models now natively support Hugging Face
